license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bb5miq46
title: Enzymatic disaggregation of human myometrium for 10X Single Cell RNA-seq
author: Aymara Mas

steps_raw.instruction: Uterine tissues should be transported in preservation solution (HypoThermosol® FRS) at 4 °C from the surgery room to the lab.
Rinse the sample with wash buffer, removing blood or mucus.
[Optional] If after centrifugation there is a ring of red blood cells, add ACKL lysis buffer, consisting in hypotonic shock, and incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00 . Then, follow step 7 again.
Add 400 µL Tryple Select Enzyme to resuspended media containing cells and mix thoroughly by pipetting. Incubate for 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 at 37 °C and centrifuge at 300 rpm, °C, .
Count the total number of cells (at least twice on two different cell counters).
Place wet tissue under a petri dish. Isolate with a sterile scalpel the myometrial layer by removing the endometrium, serosa and laser-burnt zones.
Mechanical disaggregation by chopping and thoroughly mincing up the tissue into small pieces (<1 mm 3 ).
Transfer contents to 50 ml falcon tubes containing 30 mL of enzyme buffer. Tighten lid, seal with parafilm and incubate at 37 °C overnight horizontally.
Afterwards, cell suspensions should be filtered through 100 to 50-μm polyethylene filters to remove cellular clumps and undigested tissue.
Centrifuge the filtered medium 400 x g, °C, 5 minutes . Remove supernatant and add at least 1 mL of resuspension buffer (depending of the pellet content).
Add 100 µL DNAse I to digest the extracellular genomic DNA and mix thoroughly by pipetting. Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Add at least 1 mL of resuspension buffer. Filter through a 40 μm cell strainer and centrifuge at 400 x g, °C, 5 minutes .
Remove supernatant and resuspend the content in at least 1 mL of resuspension buffer (depending of the pellet content).
[Optional] Resuspend cells in freezing medium to a concentration of 1 x 10 6 cells for storage in cryogenic vials.
